person.name: Constanze Roedig
person.bio: Key Researcher & Lecturer, Public Speaker, Creator of the Software Bill of Behavior (SBoB) and maintainer of the Kubernetes Storm Center for cloudnative intelligence Backstory: Living intentionally is hard when routine takes control. I grew up wanting to be a scientist, studying Biochemistry, starting med school, and ultimately earning a PhD in General Relativity to explore how humans perceive 'reality.' When US funding cuts ended that path, I realized academia left me without agency.In the private sector, I discovered the value of risk. The Cloud Native paradigm let me experiment, fail fast, and try again without fear of lasting damage. In positive-sum collaborations, I saw how my algorithms could shape real-world outcomes, and how taking calculated risks created visible change for people and systems alike. This freedom to test ideas with relatively low cost revealed a new kind of agency, an immediacy and measurable connection to the meaning of work.My vision is to empower others to leverage cloud native technology with respect for the human element, security, and risk. Because having agency means creating reality for our society, for each one of us, one epsilon at a time.

The following white paper is an example of this groups contribution to the community, https://www.cncf.io/reports/cloud-native-artificial-intelligence-whitepaper/.

person.name: Soto Sugita
person.bio: I am a cloud infrastructure architect who loves Kubernetes. I help customers use cloud and Kubernetes.

person.name: Anıl Gülcik
person.bio: I am a Cloud and DevOps Engineer specializing in building scalable, resilient, and production-grade cloud-native platforms. My work focuses on Kubernetes, container orchestration, CI/CD automation, infrastructure as code, and observability across modern cloud environments.  As a CNCF Kubestronaut, I am deeply engaged in the Kubernetes ecosystem and continuously expand my expertise across CNCF projects and platform engineering practices. I enjoy designing automated, developer-friendly infrastructure that improves reliability, operational efficiency, and deployment consistency.  With a multidisciplinary background and strong hands-on engineering experience, I bring a disciplined, problem-solving mindset and a collaborative approach to building and operating complex distributed systems. I am passionate about continuous learning, and exploring emerging technologies that help organizations deliver scalable and reliable platforms.

person.name: Esra Siegert
person.bio: I’m a system engineer with several years of work experience. My main focuses are Kubernetes, ArgoCD and Crossplane.

person.name: Aylin Salieva-Trendafilov
person.bio: I began my journey in the IT field about five years ago, with a primary focus on on-premises infrastructure. Currently, I’m working as a Site Reliability Engineer, driven by a passion for continuous learning and development in the ever-evolving tech landscape. I thrive on tackling new challenges and pushing myself beyond my comfort zone. I'm eager to contribute to larger-scale infrastructure projects and deepen my expertise in cloud-native environments.

person.name: Barisi Kane
person.bio: Barisi is a Senior Platform Engineer who is fascinated by cloud computing. He enjoys building scalable solutions for thorny problems.

person.name: Kerem İzgöl
person.bio: I graduated from Doğuş University in the Department of Control and Automation Engineering. I have worked in industrial automation and digital marketing companies. In 2017, my book titled “Herkes İçin Tinylab ve Arduino” (Tinylab and Arduino for Everyone) was published by Abaküs Kitap. I am currently working as a Linux system administrator in Türk Ekonomi Bankası.

person.name: Lachlan Evenson
person.bio: Lachlan is a Principal Program Manager on the open source team at Azure. As a cloud native ambassador, emeritus Kubernetes release lead, and Helm charts maintainer, Lachlan has deep operational knowledge of many Cloud Native projects. He spends his days building and contributing to software that addresses key challenges in the Cloud Native ecosystem.

person.name: Nimal Das
person.bio: Nimal Das is a Lead Product Architect with over 14 years of experience designing critical infrastructure for global enterprises. His deep expertise lies in Platform Engineering, GitOps, and Kubernetes Security, where he focuses on building resilient, scalable ecosystems. He is dedicated to bridging the gap between complex architectural requirements and developer experience, ensuring that security and scalability are built-in features of the SDLC.

person.name: Néstor Reverón
person.bio: I'm a Microsoft Technical Trainer passionate about cloud-native technologies, security, and Kubernetes. I regularly run community events and workshops in Latin America, and I'm an active member of KCD Costa Rica. I specialize in Kubernetes security (CKS), observability, and GitOps practices using tools like Prometheus, Falco, and ArgoCD. I share educational content on my LinkedIn profile and collaborate with organizations to drive CNCF adoption. Connect with me at linkedin.com/in/nestorreveron.

person.name: Yoshiya Maki
person.bio: I work as an IT infrastructure engineer at NTT Communications Corporation. I have expertise in Azure, Google Cloud, ServiceNow, Container, CI/CD, and Infrastructure as Code. I am contributing to kubernetes/website.

person.name: Adriana Villela
person.bio: Adriana is a CNCF Ambassador, blogger, host of the Geeking Out Podcast, and a maintainer of the OpenTelemetry End User SIG. By day, she's a Principal Developer Advocate at Dynatrace, focusing on Observability and OpenTelemetry. By night, she climbs walls. She also loves capybaras, because they make her happy.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Having spent a chunk of my time since 2022 working with OpenTelemetry, I'm excited to use my ambassadorship to amplify my voice to promote and generate excitement about OpenTelemetry to the tech community at large. At the same time, I love digging into other CNCF projects like Kubernetes and ArgoCD, and I am looking forward to using my voice to help spread knowledge and excitement for those projects as well!

person.name: Daniel Hawton
person.bio: Daniel is a Sr Software Developer at Solo.io with a focus in DevOps, and a maintainer with the Istio project. Prior to Solo, I was apart of SUSE Rancher.

person.name: Dineshwar Kumar
person.bio: With over 19 years of experience in IT, I specialize in Red Hat OpenShift, Red Hat Cluster, Red Hat Linux, and Kubernetes. Throughout my career, I have taken on diverse roles, ranging from Linux Support Engg to Infrastructure Architect, excelling in scripting and automation. I'm passionate about learning new open-source technologies, particularly in the areas of DevOps, DevSecOps, and cybersecurity, staying ahead of industry trends to continuously improve my skill set and contribute effectively to innovative projects.

person.name: Kaslin Fields
person.bio: Kaslin Fields is a Developer Advocate at Google Cloud, CNCF Ambassador, and a contributor to Open Source Kubernetes. She is passionate about making Cloud Native technology exciting and fun to learn for everyone from seasoned pros to total newcomers. In addition to her work supporting the development of cloud native technologies as a contributor, she also aims to spread the word about this awesome field by producing talks, videos, blogs, documentation, comics, and more.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: The Cloud Native ecosystem is active and growing, which can make it difficult for community members building awesome things to get the support they need. As an ambassador, I am passionate about providing support to folks building their own Cloud Native communities, technologies, and more.

person.name: Mangale Lopchan
person.bio: I'm a passionate Platform Engineer dedicated to building and scaling reliable systems using CNCF technologies. I share my journey and tutorials on my YouTube channel, Linux Guy Nepal, where I dive deep into Linux, Kubernetes, and cloud infrastructure. Driven by a desire for open source collaboration, I am constantly exploring new ways to enhance developer experience and operational efficiency.

person.name: Mehrdad Faridi
person.bio: Mehrdad Faridi is an accomplished Senior DevOps/Platform Engineer with over 8 years of experience in automation and cloud computing across platforms like AWS, GCP, and OpenStack. A Kubestronaut with expertise in Kubernetes, he specializes in managing resilient, cloud-native infrastructure. Mehrdad leverages CNCF tools such as ArgoCD, Prometheus, and Grafana to build scalable and efficient solutions. Known for his ability to streamline software development pipelines, he drives innovation in operational processes. His proven track record ensures high-quality outcomes in complex environments.

person.name: Sammy Cheung
person.bio: Sammy is a technology expert with over 30 years of experience in software development and systems architecture. He is an AWS Gold Jacket Ambassador, a multi-cloud certified all-star, and a Kubestronaut. Sammy is passionate about empowering people to upskill and accelerate business success in Cloud technology.

person.name: Adam Gliszczyński
person.bio: I'm a DevOps engineer with deep expertise in CNCF tooling, including production-grade Kubernetes clusters, Helm-based release management, and observability stacks built with Prometheus and Fluentd. Experienced in designing infrastructure-as-code workflows, implementing secure multi-tenant container platforms, and automating deployment pipelines across cloud-native environments.

person.name: Borislav Tonev
person.bio: I started my career 13 years ago. I have occupied various roles, from server infrastructure engineer and consultant, cloud engineer, to technical leader and manager. I strongly believe in the importance of continuous learning and knowledge sharing and I am a Microsoft Certified Trainer. I am keen on infrastructure automation and configuration management. As a manager of an infrastructure DevOps team, I have got familiar with Kubernetes as one of our main cloud native focus areas. My interests also include Argo, Envoy and Istio.

person.name: Maria Salcedo
person.bio: Backend / DevOps developer with experience in cloud native Kubernetes deployments. I am passionate about GitOps and infrastructure as code.

person.name: Shaenmugapriyan S
person.bio: I’m a DevOps Engineer with around five years of experience, working extensively with Kubernetes since 2020. I focus on cloud-native infrastructure and CNCF technologies, with a strong emphasis on multicloud Kubernetes operations. I’ve spoken at the Google Cloud Community Chennai on multicloud cluster management using Anthos and on migrating VM-based workloads to Amazon EKS.

person.name: Shogo Shimahara
person.bio: Engineer based in Japan.  I have strong passion for learning cloud-native technologies. My current work involves advising usage and troubleshooting technical issues for managed Kubernetes and container-based services on Microsoft Azure.

person.name: Willem Berroubache
person.bio: As a Security Architect at a leading telecom company in Europe, I oversee an essential project aimed at strengthening the security framework of 5G networks, with a focus on Telco Cloud infrastructure.  My role is to actively promote cloud-native technologies within the company, advocating for their strategic implementation to enhance our services.  In addition to my project management duties, I am committed to regularly training my colleagues on Kubernetes, Linux, Prometheus, cloud-native ecosystems, and observability practices, ensuring that our team remains at the cutting edge of industry developments.  As a cloud technology enthusiast, I am constantly seeking ways to improve my skills and deepen my knowledge in these technologies, as well as other rapidly evolving fields.

person.name: Bhavani Indukuri
person.bio: CNCF. She possesses extensive expertise in optimising Release processes and DevOps methodologies at Zscaler. In her previous role at Servicenow, Bhavani spearheaded a team in migrating infrastructure to Kubernetes, skilfully managing microservice deployments, Kubernetes application backup and monitoring.  Recognised as a prominent speaker at conferences, Bhavani is passionate about enlightening audiences on the transformative potential of DevOps and CNCF Projects. Through her involvement with Women in CNCF, she orchestrates workshops focused on CNCF projects and advocates for increased female representation in the CNCF ecosystem.

person.name: Bryant Yu
person.bio: A passionated solution builder enjoys turning innovative ideas into real-world solutions; growing PoC cases into Enterprise production environments solving challenging questions.  Skilled in hybrid-cloud and AI/ML applications development. Keen interest in new technologies and a Never-Stop learner.

person.name: Fog Dong
person.bio: Fog Dong, a maintainer of KubeVela, and a senior engineer at Alibaba Cloud. She has been actively involved in the open source community, particularly in the cloud native DevOps field. Fog is now responsible for building large scale application workflow for Serverless Application Engine Product in Alibaba, while she's also fully experienced on building cloud native CI/CD platform when worked at Bytedance.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: As an Ambassador of CNCF, I am excited to have the opportunity to promote and educate people about cloud-native technologies, which are shaping the future of computing. CNCF has a vibrant community of developers and tech enthusiasts who are passionate about advancing these technologies, and I am thrilled to be a part of this community. Additionally, as an ambassador, I will have the chance to connect with other like-minded individuals and share knowledge and ideas, which will help us all stay up-to-date with the latest developments in this rapidly-evolving field. Overall, I am honored to represent CNCF and am looking forward to contributing to the growth and success of this innovative organization.

person.name: Konstantinos Stavropoulos
person.bio: I have been working with Containers & other Cloud Native technologies since 2020.  I am passionate about Infrastructure-As-Code, Policy-As-Code and actually everything-as-code, as I believe that it helps humans, teams and organizations to operate fast and securely.At work, I like automating as much as possible -following GitOps practices-, ensuring Supply Chain Security from CI/CD pipelines and IaC to Kubernetes clusters and Containers, and setting up observability tools and alerts.  Outside of tech I enjoy hiking, literature, 3D printing, playing the guitar, and being part of Open Source communities - contributing anywhere I can.

person.name: Masaki Hagiwara
person.bio: I work for Microsoft as a Support for Mission Critical Cloud Solution Architect.  My area of expertise is Azure infrastructure, but I have a background as a pre-sales engineer for a hardware manufacturer, so I am particularly good at hybrid cloud computing. I am constantly honing my skills so that I can contribute to improving IT systems.
